target.name: StoreDot
target.founder: Doron Myersdorf, Simon Litsyn, Gil Rosenman

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $206M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
StoreDot, a Series E climate and energy company, has raised $206.5M to date. The relevant comp universe spans 9 private peers (including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape) and 1 public comparable (Linamar, ticker LNZA), though none provide sufficiently tight peer alignment for traditional multiples-based valuation.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, given the absence of a cohesive narrow peer set in Climate & Energy at StoreDot's stage and scale. Using total capital raised ($206M) as a valuation proxy and applying a standard Series E-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.4x yields an implied valuation of $289M. This method captures typical venture step-up discipline but depends on the assumption that raised capital reasonably proxies pre-money valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $246M to $332M, centered on $289M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of same-stage peer data to validate step-up assumptions specific to frontier energy, and reliance on a single methodological anchor rather than triangulation. Resolution of these gaps would require either comparable transaction data for adjacent-stage Climate & Energy rounds or public market trading multiples that more closely track StoreDot's technology and market profile.
